market_summary: Mixed-to-risk-on regime for the 30-day horizon: SPY +8.46%, QQQ +11.55%, and IWM +10.75% over 1m, all above MA50/MA200; TLT remains below both trend measures, which supports ongoing equity leadership.

Leadership still favors AI/semicap and internet beta: AVGO +31.94% 1m, AMZN +23.08%, GOOGL +16.68%, KLAC +15.91%, NVDA +13.95%, and TSM +11.48%, all with positive trend support; XOM is the clearest short-term laggard at -8.98% 1m and below MA50, while COST is positive but slow at +4.17% 1m.
No-go for rebalance today: cadence is not due, execution policy requires no trade, and the qualitative refresh did not verify severe new thesis damage strong enough to override spacing discipline.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Pack shows the weakest short-term tape in the book: -8.98% over 1m, below MA50, and lagging SPY by 17.44 percentage points over the same window.
Defensive ballast is intact but alpha drag persists: +4.17% over 1m versus SPY +8.46% and QQQ +11.55%; qualitative follow-up did not verify a fresh positive catalyst.
No direct qualitative damage verified, but key-fundamental/news follow-up beyond pack momentum is UNKNOWN for this run.

thesis: Catalyst: none sufficient for an off-cycle paper trade; the strongest holdings remain concentrated in semicap/AI and internet leadership, while only XOM shows clear short-term technical slippage.

Factor edge: turnover discipline is the edge today because the existing book remains aligned with the prevailing risk-on tape; six of eight holdings still have positive 1m momentum and sit above MA50/MA200.

Variant view: the market could continue broadening beyond current AI leaders, but absent verified thesis break or superior qualitatively-cleared replacement evidence today, preserving the current portfolio is the higher-confidence choice.
risks: Macro: a rates backup, growth scare, or commodity shock could rotate leadership away from current tech-heavy exposures or deepen weakness in XOM.

Idiosyncratic: semiconductor concentration leaves the book exposed to earnings execution, capex digestion, export-policy, and supply-chain risk; qualitative updates for most holdings are UNKNOWN this run.
Execution/liquidity: doing nothing avoids unnecessary turnover and slippage, but it also preserves exposure to the portfolio's weaker names if relative weakness persists into the next checkpoint.
falsifiable_checks: By the next scheduled rebalance checkpoint, if XOM remains below MA50 and still trails SPY on the full window from 2026-04-24 by more than 10 percentage points, elevate it to highest replacement priority.

By the next weekly checkpoint, if COST's 1m return remains below SPY by more than 5 percentage points and a non-held candidate still has stronger pack momentum with no qualitative damage, revisit the no-trade stance.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: risk-on conditions persist, breadth is supportive, and the current portfolio is still mostly aligned with leadership, so expected benefit from an off-cycle trade does not clear trading-cost drag. Bull: AVGO, KLAC, TSM, GOOGL, NVDA, and AMZN continue to carry the book with strong relative momentum. Bear: XOM and COST remain the weak links, but the evidence today is not strong enough to justify breaking weekly spacing discipline in a 30-day paper process.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights=KLAC/AVGO/TSM/GOOGL strongest on pack momentum; XOM weakest short-term, COST low-beta laggard; qualitative fundamentals/news largely UNKNOWN except NVDA quality snapshot strong (GM 71.1%, net margin 55.6%, ROE 76.3%, ROIC 92.6%). Risk budget=IT 53%, no position >15%, no crypto, diversified across 5 sectors. Scenario stress=equity index shock hits semis most; rates shock pressures long-duration growth but breadth cushion remains; commodity shock hurts XOM/helps inflation hedges not owned; USD shock mainly ADR sensitivity in TSM. Execution plan=no trade, preserve portfolio exactly, reassess at next scheduled weekly checkpoint. Monitoring/kill-switch=XOM persistent MA50 failure and >10pt SPY lag; COST relative lag >5pt vs SPY; any verified earnings/guidance/regulatory thesis break in core AI holdings; missing qualitative verification lowers confidence.
